title: Shoreline
tagline: Full-stack hospitality management platform with analytics.
description: Shoreline is a restaurant and hospitality management platform featuring reservation tracking, staff scheduling, inventory control, and a live analytics dashboard — built with Flask and a PostgreSQL backend.
story: Inspired by the chaos of watching a beachside restaurant try to manage reservations on a whiteboard and staff schedules on a shared Google Sheet. Shoreline was built to be the single source of truth for a hospitality operation.
journey: Shoreline started as a direct response to a real problem I watched play out in an actual kitchen — paper schedules, whiteboard reservations, no single system. I built the first version in Flask to prove the concept worked. After watching operators use it, the technical debt became obvious: the monolith needed to become an API. v5 is that rethink — a ground-up rebuild designed to scale from one location to a regional chain without rewiring everything.
forWho: Restaurant managers and hospitality operators who need a unified back-office system without the enterprise price tag.

detailedSpecs.architecture: Monolithic Flask app with Blueprint-based routing. PostgreSQL for persistent data, Alembic for schema versioning.
keyPatterns: Blueprint modularization per domain (reservations, staff, inventory)
Alembic migration chain for zero-downtime schema changes
Chart.js dashboard fed by Flask API endpoints

futureBranch.label: v5
futureBranch.tagline: The next generation — rebuilt with a modern API-first architecture.
futureBranch.description: Shoreline v5 is a ground-up rebuild transitioning from a monolithic Flask app to a clean REST API backend with a decoupled frontend, improved JWT auth, and a fully redesigned multi-tenant data model.

detailedSpecs.architecture: Decoupled REST API (Flask) + headless frontend. JWT-based auth replacing sessions. Multi-tenant schema design with org-scoped data.
keyPatterns: CI/CD pipeline with automated migration runs
